Title: Structure of ribosome-bound azole-modified peptide phazolicin rationalizes its species-specific mode of bacterial translation inhibition

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in S. meliloti Sm1021
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: External PHZ exposure in MIC assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Plasmid-borne expression in S. meliloti; MIC assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers strong resistance to PHZ and mediates self-immunity by export
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used as a selectable resistance/self-immunity module in PHZ-producing or PHZ-exposed strains
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression increased the MIC by more than 100-fold compared with empty vector control, supporting exporter-based immunity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzE encodes a transporter associated with the PHZ biosynthetic cluster. 2. Heterologous expression of phzE in S. meliloti Sm1021 conferred strong resistance to external PHZ. 3. phzE therefore functions in self-immunity, most likely by exporting PHZ.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Plasmid-borne expression of WT and mutant alleles in S. meliloti
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth on medium containing 8× MIC PHZ
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous/plasmid expression; growth resistance assay; cryo-EM structural analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A Tth-like G68H substitution in uL4 confers PHZ resistance, showing that uL4 loop sequence determines species specificity of PHZ action
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing the uL4 loop could engineer PHZ resistance or define target specificity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The K65A substitution did not confer resistance, indicating that steric exclusion by the loop residue corresponding to Gly64/Gly68 is more decisive than loss of a single hydrogen bond
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rplD, encoding ribosomal protein uL4, forms part of the PHZ binding site in the ribosomal exit tunnel. 2. Structural comparison suggested that bulky uL4 residues can sterically clash with PHZ. 3. In S. meliloti, the Tth-like G68H mutation conferred resistance, establishing uL4 loop sequence as a determinant of species-specific PHZ sensitivity.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Plasmid-borne expression of WT and mutant allele in S. meliloti
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth on medium containing 8× MIC PHZ
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; plasmid expression; growth resistance assay; structural comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: uL22 contributes to the PHZ binding site, but the K90R substitution did not confer resistance, indicating it is not a major determinant of specificity under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less promising than uL4 as a resistance engineering target for PHZ specificity manipulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Structural data suggested possible steric involvement of the corresponding Tth Arg90 residue, but functional testing in S. meliloti did not support uL22 K90 as decisive
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rplV, encoding ribosomal protein uL22, also lies at the NPET constriction adjacent to bound PHZ. 2. Structural comparison suggested possible clash effects in some species. 3. However, the tested K90R substitution in S. meliloti did not confer PHZ resistance, so uL22 was not established as the principal specificity determinant.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Linear azol(in)e-containing peptide (LAP) core peptide formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Phazolicin precursor core that is post-translationally modified into PHZ
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptide (RiPP); linear azole-containing peptide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Narrow-spectrum antibacterial peptide that inhibits bacterial translation elongation by obstructing the ribosomal nascent peptide exit tunnel
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cultivation of Rhizobium sp. Pop5 in RM medium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Compound purification from producer strain; MALDI-TOF-MS; high-resolution MS; ESI-MS/MS and MALDI-MS/MS structural analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes the precursor peptide required for production of phazolicin, the antibacterial end product
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct structural precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering PHZ biosynthesis or related LAP production systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining / PSI-BLAST-based biosynthetic gene cluster identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The mature PHZ derives from the C-terminal core region after leader peptide cleavage and installation of eight azole cycles; alternative longer mature forms A-PHZ and TA-PHZ were also detected
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzA encodes the precursor peptide of phazolicin in the newly identified phzEACBD cluster. 2. Mass spectrometry supported that the mature PHZ product is derived from the PhzA core peptide after leader cleavage and extensive azole modification. 3. The resulting PHZ is a translation-inhibiting LAP with antibacterial activity against selected Rhizobiales.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LAP partner protein for cyclodehydratase complex
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: PhzA precursor peptide together with cyclodehydratase machinery
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Azoline-containing intermediate en route to PHZ
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Partner component assisting cyclodehydration of Cys/Ser/Thr residues in the precursor peptide to form azoline heterocycles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatic biosynthetic gene cluster analysis; pathway inference from conserved LAP machinery
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to contribute to azole heterocycle formation required for PHZ maturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for modification of precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate component for reconstructing PHZ biosynthesis in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining / PSI-BLAST-based biosynthetic gene cluster identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function assigned by homology to LAP biosynthetic clusters; not directly biochemically assayed in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzC is part of the newly discovered phzEACBD biosynthetic cluster. 2. By homology to known LAP systems, it likely acts with PhzD to cyclize precursor residues. 3. Its role supports formation of the azole-rich PHZ scaffold.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes azoline heterocycles in the modified precursor peptide to azoles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatic biosynthetic gene cluster analysis; product structure inference from MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to generate the mature azole state needed for fully modified PHZ
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms structural features of target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction of PHZ/LAP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining / PSI-BLAST-based biosynthetic gene cluster identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned by conserved LAP BGC organization and the observed fully oxidized azole rings in PHZ
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzB belongs to the PHZ biosynthetic cluster and is homologous to LAP FMN-dependent dehydrogenases. 2. The mature PHZ structure contains multiple azoles consistent with a PhzB-catalyzed oxidation step. 3. phzB is therefore inferred to be essential for PHZ maturation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent cyclodehydration of Cys/Ser/Thr residues in the precursor peptide to generate azoline heterocycles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatic biosynthetic gene cluster analysis; product structure inference from MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to install precursor heterocycles essential for PHZ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate heterocyclic intermediates toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key catalytic module for synthetic biology production of PHZ-like LAPs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining / PSI-BLAST-based biosynthetic gene cluster identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from conserved YcaO-dependent LAP biosynthesis logic; not directly mutated here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzD is a core component of the newly identified phazolicin biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. By homology to known LAP pathways, PhzD likely cyclodehydrates selected Cys/Ser/Thr residues in PhzA. 3. This step underlies installation of the heterocyclic scaffold of PHZ.
